_note: Phase 10 attempted to capture the mutation score on src/engine/{flows,atoms,composites,adapters}/**/*.ts. The active sandbox (greywall) blocks Stryker's logging-server TCP-IPC: child workers connect via net.createConnection(port, 'localhost') and greywall denies the syscall with EPERM on internalConnectMultiple (both ::1 and 127.0.0.1). The documented `vitest pool: 'forks'` workaround does not address this: it covers vitest worker IPC, not Stryker core's logging-server. RIDGELINE_SANDBOX=0 / GREYWALL_SANDBOX=0 do not disable greywall (kernel-level enforcement). To capture this score, run `npx stryker run` on the host outside greywall, then run `node scripts/phase-10-record-newscore.mjs .check/mutation.json`. See .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/phase-10-stryker-environment.md for the full diagnosis.

stryker_runtime_error.stage: ChildProcessProxy — logging-server connect from forked worker
stryker_runtime_error.fatal_log_excerpt: Unexpected error occurred while running Stryker StrykerError: AggregateError: EPERM (undefined) AggregateError [EPERM] at internalConnectMultiple (node:net:1193:18); root: connect EPERM ::1:<port> and 127.0.0.1:<port> (Local 0.0.0.0:0)

_phase_11_dependency: AC4 of Phase 10 cannot assert numerically until both this file and baseline/mutation-score.json have captured: true. The gate at scripts/phase-10-mutation-gate.mjs returns deferred (exit 0) until then; once captured, it asserts new_score >= baseline_score and fails with exit 1 on regression.
